usage: framework-agnostic demo content and UI-state sample data

login.hint: No real SMS or backend. Keep the flow credible, but lightweight.
login.footer: The first successful login routes into the home shell with the Chats tab active.
login.validationMessage: Please enter a complete demo phone number before continuing.
login.validationHint: Inline validation should be calm, visible, and intentional.
login.validationFooter: Use concise feedback and keep the rest of the form stable when validation fails.

conversations.title: Alex Mercer
conversations.preview: The design pass looks solid. Ship the home shell with tabs visible in MVP.

conversations.title: Team Sync
conversations.preview: Need parity notes for CJMP, KMP, and flutter after the next round.

conversations.title: Mina Park
conversations.preview: We can keep Contacts and Settings shallow for now, but they should be present.

conversations.title: Product Review
conversations.preview: Customer demo quality matters more than feature count for the benchmark.

conversations.title: Support Ops
conversations.preview: The loading, empty, and send-pending states should feel production-credible.

loadingState.title: Loading conversations
loadingState.body: Use coherent, non-janky loading states instead of blank scaffolds.

emptyState.title: No chats yet
emptyState.body: The empty state should still feel like a product surface, not a broken screen.

contactsSurface.title: Contacts stays lightweight in MVP
contactsSurface.body: The tab exists to keep the product surface close to Telegram. Detailed contacts management is intentionally deferred.

messages.text: Let's keep the MVP narrow, but the surface should still feel close to Telegram.
Agreed. Home shell, chats, detail, and send flow should all feel believable.
Also keep Contacts and Settings visible in the shell, even if they stay shallow.
That keeps the demo credible without exploding the implementation scope.

pendingState.draftText: Shipping the MVP shell design now...
pendingState.messageText: Shipping the MVP shell design now. You can validate parity after implementation.
